verdict_reasoning: EREG is a member of the EGF family with a TM-precursor architecture. The gene encodes a single-pass transmembrane proligand that is proteolytically cleaved by sheddases (TACE/ADAM17 and other metalloproteases) to release an active soluble ectodomain. The TM-anchored precursor stage is transient and the shed soluble form is the dominant biological actor in EGFR/ERBB4 signaling; however, the protein does transit the plasma membrane as a membrane-anchored precursor before regulated shedding. Cell-surface biotinylation and surface-proteomics studies detect the TM form, even though bulk sequencing captures primarily the cleaved fragment. The surface-accessible stage is real but short-lived and minority relative to the soluble pool.
